title: Creamy Italian Chestnut Trofie
description: A buttery and pillowy Italian dish from Piedmont, featuring chestnuts and dumplings, perfect for a sick day. This raw-friendly trofie recipe is a comforting and delicious meal.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour and eggs to make the trofie dough.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and let it rest for 30 minutes.
Roll out the dough to a thickness of 1/4 inch and cut it into trofie shapes.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the trofie for 3-5 minutes, or until they float to the surface.
In a large skillet, melt the butter over medium heat and add the chopped chestnuts.
Cook the chestnuts for 5 minutes, or until they are lightly browned and fragrant.
Add the heavy cream to the skillet and stir to combine with the chestnuts and butter.
Bring the cream mixture to a simmer and let it cook for 2-3 minutes, or until it has thickened slightly.
Add the cooked trofie to the cream mixture and toss to combine with the chestnuts and sauce.
Season the trofie with salt, black pepper, and grated grana padano cheese.
